display.purpose: h1-h3, page titles, the Dashboard greeting.

body.purpose: Body copy, labels, buttons, all non-heading text.

japanese.purpose: Every Japanese-language string, via the .jp-text utility.

shadows.name: glow-new-rest
shadows.purpose: Quiet always-on tint marking a 'New' word/kanji card.

shadows.name: glow-new-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus intensification of the New glow.

shadows.name: glow-practice-rest
shadows.purpose: Quiet always-on tint marking a 'Practice' card.

shadows.name: glow-review-rest
shadows.purpose: Quiet always-on tint marking a 'Review due' card.

shadows.name: glow-mastered-rest
shadows.purpose: Quiet always-on tint marking a 'Mastered' card.

shadows.name: cta-ledge
shadows.purpose: The primary button's solid pressed-ledge, removed entirely on :active.

shadows.name: celebration-overlay
shadows.purpose: The Level-Up dialog's overlay — the one moment the system goes darker/heavier than its normal flat language.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition easing app-wide (Tailwind's default transition-all).

motion.name: press-depth
motion.purpose: The primary button's tactile lift-then-flatten interaction.

motion.name: sliding-indicator
motion.purpose: SegmentedTabs' pill indicator gliding to the active option's measured position.

components.name: Primary Button (CTA)
components.description: The one primary action per screen — Lantern Violet gradient with a solid pressed ledge.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><polygon points="5 3 19 12 5 21 5 3"/></svg>Start Study Session</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; border-radius: 1.8rem; padding: 10px 20px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: #ffffff; background-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, #6460e5, #5050d5); box-shadow: 0 4px 0 0 #3d3aa8, inset 0 1.5px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.35); border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms, filter 150ms, box-shadow 150ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(4px); box-shadow: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4c6ef0; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: The default flat container — hairline border, no shadow at rest.
components.html:
Today's Path

Grammar · 15 min remaining
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0f172a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border-radius: 1.8rem; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: 'Baloo 2', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; color: #f1f5f9; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-size: 14px; color: #94a3b8; }

components.name: Learning State Badge (New)
components.description: A semantic pill — color is locked to meaning (blue=new, violet=practice, amber=review, emerald=mastered).
components.html: New
components.css: .ds-badge-new { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; background: rgba(59,130,246,0.15); color: #93c5fd; }

components.name: Segmented Tab (level filter)
components.description: A bordered track with a sliding indigo pill indicator — the app's signature filter/toggle control.
components.css: .ds-seg-track { position: relative; display: inline-flex; border-radius: 1rem; border: 1px solid #1e293b; background: #0f172a; padding: 4px; } .ds-seg-indicator { position: absolute; top: 4px; left: 4px; width: 48px; height: calc(100% - 8px); border-radius: 0.8rem; background: #3a54d6; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(58,84,214,0.3), 0 0 16px -2px rgba(58,84,214,0.7); transition: all 200ms ease-out; } .ds-seg-item { position: relative; z-index: 1; padding: 6px 16px; border: none; background: transparent; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: #94a3b8; cursor: pointer; } .ds-seg-active { color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Ring Stat
components.description: A circular SVG progress ring showing a real, bounded ratio (streak vs. best, minutes vs. goal) with a centered icon — never decorative or fixed-arc.
components.html: 🔥
components.css: .ds-ring { position: relative; width: 56px; height: 56px; color: #1e293b; } .ds-ring-icon { position: absolute; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 20px; }

narrative.overview: Kotobox is a small, steady light held up in a dark, star-lit room — the mascot lantern-in-hand, guiding a learner through one calm study session toward a real destination: genuine JLPT readiness. Every night-sky hero panel, every glowing ring and card border, is that same lantern-light: present, warm, low-key. It never shouts for attention, because the destination (being exam-ready) is the point, not the light itself. This system explicitly rejects the "carnival" reading of gamification: loud confetti-everywhere, neon progress bars, mascots that upstage the content, flat gray/white corporate dashboard chrome, and any direct visual cloning of a single competitor. Streaks, XP, levels, and badges exist here, but they are supporting instruments — quiet indicators of real progress, not the show.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, night-sky atmosphere as the default stage; light mode is a fully-supported second reading of the same system, not an afterthought.
Color communicates meaning, never decoration — a small, closed vocabulary reused everywhere rather than expanding per screen.
Depth comes from soft colored glow, not gray elevation shadows.
The mascot is present at key moments but is a companion in the scene, not a mascot-first interface.

rules.name: The Closed Vocabulary Rule
rules.body: No new named colors get introduced for a single screen. If a new UI element needs color, it draws from the existing set or it doesn't get color at all.

rules.name: The One Button Rule
rules.body: Lantern Violet appears on at most one button per screen — the single primary action.

rules.name: The Same Color, Same Meaning Rule
rules.body: The four learning-state colors are locked to their meanings everywhere they appear: card border, glow, badge, and progress-bar fill all agree.

rules.name: The Fluid-Headline Rule
rules.body: Only headline-level text uses clamp() fluid sizing. Helper/label text stays at fixed Tailwind steps.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Gray Rule
rules.body: If an element needs to feel lifted or important, reach for a colored glow tied to its semantic meaning before reaching for a gray drop shadow.

dos: Do keep Lantern Violet to exactly one button per screen — the single primary action.
Do let the four learning-state colors mean the same thing everywhere: card border, glow, badge, and progress-bar fill all agree.
Do use colored glow, not gray drop shadow, whenever a card needs to feel emphasized or stateful.
Do render every animation with a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce fallback.
Do keep every displayed number real — derived from actual stored progress, never a placeholder.
Do reserve the mascot for specific moments rather than putting it on every surface.
donts: Don't ship 'carnival' gamification — confetti, sound, or motion that overshadows the actual studying.
Don't clone Duolingo's specific visual identity directly.
Don't default to flat gray/white corporate SaaS dashboard chrome.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient headlines, side-stripe colored borders, or glassmorphism as a default decorative treatment.
Don't introduce a new named color for a single screen.
Don't use a gray elevation shadow where a semantic colored glow would communicate more.
Don't add dead buttons, fake data, or placeholder features without an honest lock/disabled treatment.
